target.name: Altos Labs
target.founder: Hal Barron, Rick Klausner, Hans Bishop

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Altos Labs, a late-stage biotech company founded by Hal Barron, Rick Klausner, and Hans Bishop, has raised $3.0B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, though the peer set exhibits limited direct comparability at identical stage and scale.

Stage-progression valuation, the primary applicable method, applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement to the $3.0B raised proxy, implying $3.6B. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds (fewer than three datapoints in narrow filters). Public-comp anchoring using the 3-ticker Biotech & Health P/S median of 6.1x could not be applied because target revenue is not disclosed, a common constraint for private companies. The public comp tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) provide sector context but insufficient direct leverage for valuation without revenue visibility.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.1B to $4.1B, centered at $3.6B. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable late-stage biotech exits in the immediate peer set, lack of revenue or profitability metrics, and limited disclosed operational milestones. The valuation relies substantially on capital-raised as a proxy and typical stage-transition multiples rather than fundamental operating metrics.
